widgets_values: sky, effects, cubemaps, skyrim.exm, tintmasks, facedetails, terrain, shadertests, interface

nodes.title: 🎨 Core / Most Common Texture Suffixes
widgets_values:
_d.dds → Diffuse (albedo/color)
_n.dds → Normal map
_msn.dds → Model Space Normal (used a lot in Skyrim)
_s.dds → Specular map (shininess)
_g.dds → Glow / emissive
_e.dds → Environment mask (cubemap influence)

🧱 PBR-ish / Extended Maps (mostly SSE / mods)
_m.dds → Metalness (rare, modded setups)
_r.dds → Roughness (very modded)
_ao.dds → Ambient Occlusion
_p.dds → Parallax map
_h.dds → Height map (parallax/terrain)
_b.dds → Blend map (terrain or layered materials)

🌍 Landscape / Terrain
_landscape.dds → Base terrain diffuse
_lod.dds → Level of Detail texture
_far.dds → Distant terrain
_noise.dds → Noise texture (terrain blending)

🧍 Actors / Skin / Characters
_sk.dds → Skin map (subsurface tinting)
_s.dds → Specular (very important for skin)
_msn.dds → Face/body normals
_detail.dds → Detail overlay (pores, etc.)

🧩 Multi-layer / Variants
_01.dds, _02.dds, _03.dds → Variants
_a.dds, _b.dds, _c.dds → Alternate versions
_mask.dds → Mask texture
_alpha.dds → Alpha mask

🏗️ Architecture / Objects
_cube.dds → Cubemap (reflection)
_env.dds → Environment map
_emit.dds → Emissive (alt naming for glow)
_detail.dds → Detail texture overlay

🌊 Water / Effects
_flow.dds → Flow map (water/lava movement)
_foam.dds → Foam texture
_caustics.dds → Light caustics

🌿 Grass / Trees
_billboard.dds → Billboard LOD texture
_leaf.dds → Leaves (sometimes custom naming)

🧪 Misc / Engine-specific quirks
_dp.dds → Detail parallax (rare)
_sr.dds → Specular + reflectivity packed
_mask_m.dds → Packed mask maps (modded pipelines)
_blend.dds → Texture blending
_subsurface.dds → Fake SSS (modded shaders)

⚠️ Important Skyrim-specific quirks
_msn.dds is NOT the same as _n.dds
_msn = model space normals (RGB = direction)
_n = tangent space normals (more standard)
Specular is often packed in alpha channel of _n.dds or _d.dds in vanilla assets
Naming is not strictly enforced by the engine
→ shaders + NIF material paths define usage
